aliases: Paneer Tikka Gobi Kebab Mushroom Tikka Platter

english: Paneer Tikka Gobi Kebab Mushroom Tikka Platter

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists for a mixed tandoori vegetable platter specifically; FOODON_03315091 (tandoori food product) used as closest parent.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKP (mixed vegetable dish) used as closest parent; no specific code exists for a tandoori mixed vegetable platter combination.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept found for tandoori mixed vegetable platter.

main_ingredients: spices (cumin, coriander, turmeric, chilli, garam masala)
allergens: gluten (possible in veg seekh kebab binding)

serving.natural_language_portion: One tandoori veg platter typically weighs approximately 400 g and is intended as a shared appetiser for two to three people, comprising portions of paneer tikka, tandoori gobi, veg seekh kebab, and mushroom tikka.

nutrient_source.title: Nutrient values estimated by weighted component averaging of ICMR-NIN published values for paneer tikka, tandoori gobi, vegetable seekh kebab, and mushroom tikka
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition), Hyderabad, India; supplemented by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy for mushroom and cauliflower raw ingredient reference
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No single ICMR-NIN entry exists for a combined tandoori veg platter. Values derived by equal-weight averaging of four component dishes: paneer tikka (~25% weight share), tandoori gobi (~25%), veg seekh kebab (~25%), mushroom tikka (~25%). ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 edition) used for paneer tikka and vegetable kebab values; USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy used for mushroom and cauliflower reference cross-check. Confidence is medium due to component estimation.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index estimated from analogous tandoori/grilled mixed vegetable and paneer dishes; reference anchored to published GI values for paneer-based dishes (~35–40) and mixed spiced vegetable preparations (~45–55) from the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283.
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for a combined tandoori veg platter. GI of 45 estimated as a weighted average of component analogues: paneer tikka GI ~35 (dairy-protein dominant, low GI per University of Sydney database for paneer/cottage cheese dishes), tandoori gobi GI ~50 (spiced cauliflower, low GI vegetable), veg seekh kebab GI ~50 (mixed vegetable and legume binding, moderate-low), mushroom tikka GI ~40 (mushroom, very low GI). Weighted average yields approximately 44–46; GI set to 45. GL per serving = (45 × 11.5 × 400) / (100 × 100) = 20.7 g. gi_category is low (GI ≤ 55).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~420 mg per 100 g; ~1680 mg per platter serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that a full tandoori veg platter provides approximately 1680 mg sodium per serving, representing over 70% of the recommended daily limit of 2300 mg; portion control and requesting reduced-salt marinades are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index (~45) and moderate carbohydrate content (~11.5 g per 100 g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: The tandoori veg platter has a low estimated glycemic index of approximately 45, driven by high protein from paneer and fibre from vegetables, making it a suitable appetiser choice for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when consumed in moderate portions; the glycemic load per platter serving is approximately 20.7 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from paneer and yoghurt marinade (~3.4 g per 100 g) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The platter contains moderate saturated fat (~13.6 g per full serving) primarily from paneer and yoghurt, and high sodium (~1680 mg per serving); individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit portion size and prefer mushroom tikka and tandoori gobi components over paneer-heavy portions to reduce saturated fat intake.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Tandoori Veg Platter is a popular Indian vegetarian appetiser platter consisting of four distinct tandoor-cooked items: paneer tikka (cubes of Indian cottage cheese marinated in spiced yoghurt), tandoori gobi (whole or halved cauliflower florets marinated in turmeric and chilli yoghurt), veg seekh kebab (minced mixed vegetables and chickpea flour shaped onto skewers), and mushroom tikka (button mushrooms marinated in spiced yoghurt). All components are cooked in a traditional clay tandoor oven at high heat (typically 400–480°C), which imparts a characteristic smoky char and caramelised exterior while retaining moisture inside. The platter is typically served with mint chutney, sliced onions, lemon wedges, and sometimes a small portion of naan or roti. The flavour profile is smoky, mildly spicy, tangy from yoghurt and lemon, with earthy notes from cumin and coriander. Texturally, paneer is firm and slightly chewy, gobi is tender with charred edges, seekh kebab is soft and crumbly, and mushroom tikka is juicy and meaty. Nutritionally, the platter provides moderate protein (~7.8 g per 100 g) primarily from paneer and chickpea flour, moderate fat (~8.2 g per 100 g) from dairy and cooking oil, and relatively low carbohydrate (~11.5 g per 100 g). The estimated glycemic index is low (~45), making it a favourable choice for blood glucose management. Sodium content is elevated due to spice marinades and salt, warranting attention for hypertensive individuals. The dish is vegetarian, halal-friendly, and widely available in Indian restaurants across Singapore, including at Mr Tandoor, Boat Quay. It is suitable as a shared starter for two to three people.
common_user_queries: What is in a tandoori veg platter?
Is tandoori veg platter good for diabetics?
Paneer tikka gobi mushroom tikka platter nutrition

confidence_reason: No single approved-source entry exists for a combined tandoori veg platter. Nutrient values were estimated by equal-weight component averaging of ICMR-NIN published values for the four constituent dishes (paneer tikka, tandoori gobi, veg seekh kebab, mushroom tikka). GI was estimated from analogous University of Sydney GI Database entries for paneer and mixed vegetable dishes. Confidence is medium rather than low because the component sources are primary approved sources (ICMR-NIN, USDA SR Legacy) and the estimation methodology is transparent and defensible.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-estimated from ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017) using equal 25% weight shares for each of the four platter components. Actual restaurant preparation may vary. 2. Sodium value (~420 mg/100 g) reflects typical spiced yoghurt marinade and salt usage in tandoori preparations; restaurant-specific values may be higher. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these component dishes. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in ICMR-NIN for these component dishes. 5. GI of 45 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this combined platter exists in the University of Sydney GI Database. 6. The platter is served at Mr Tandoor, Boat Quay, Singapore (field survey data source record GD002908). 7. Price SGD 36.90 per platter as recorded in source data. 8. trans_fat_g of 0.1 reflects trace levels from dairy-derived conjugated linoleic acid in paneer and yoghurt; this is a naturally occurring trace amount, not industrial trans fat. 9. Allergen note: veg seekh kebab may contain gluten if wheat flour is used as a binder; paneer and yoghurt contain milk allergens. 10. This is an Indian cuisine dish served in Singapore; multilingual names are provided in Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, and Hindi per Singaporean multilingual naming requirements.
